description: Build and record a concrete dmscs identifier and naming inventory for the CMS PostgreSQL install path before changing scripts.
acceptance-criteria: Every SQL file under src/config/backend/EdFi.DmsConfigurationService.Backend.Postgresql/Deploy/Scripts is accounted for as schema, seed, tenant-alter, OpenIddict, or extension setup work.
Each existing dmscs primary key, foreign key, check, unique constraint, unique index, and lookup index is mapped to the intended PK_*, FK_*, CK_*, UX_*, or IX_* name.
Tenant-owned logical uniqueness is identified from repository behavior and table ownership, including where TenantId must be part of a UX_* constraint.
OpenIddict tables and currently implicit primary key or unique constraints are included in the inventory.
The inventory distinguishes logical UX_* constraints from non-unique IX_* lookup indexes and flags redundant unique indexes for removal.
The inventory is captured in a new dmscs-identifier-and-naming-inventory.md file committed with the implementation so later tasks do not depend on memory, and the file is referenced in progress.txt
steps-to-verify: Review the planned names against DMS generated naming patterns in src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Constraints/ConstraintNaming.cs

description: Update core CMS PostgreSQL create-table scripts to use explicit quoted PascalCase dmscs identifiers and guarded named constraints.
acceptance-criteria: Core CMS tables such as Vendor, Application, ApiClient, ClaimSet, AuthorizationStrategy, ResourceClaim, ClaimsHierarchy, DataStore, DataStoreContext, DataStoreDerivative, Tenant, Profile, ProfileApplication, and ApiClientDataStore are created as quoted PascalCase objects under "dmscs".
All table columns, comments, references, and ALTER TABLE statements use explicit quoted PascalCase identifiers.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS remains in place for fresh quoted-schema idempotency.
PK_*, FK_*, UX_*, IX_*, and CK_* names follow the story convention and FK names use target names unless a role is needed to disambiguate.
PK, FK, UX, and CK constraints are added with guarded ALTER TABLE blocks using pg_constraint checks against quoted table regclass values rather than relying on inline constraints for idempotency.
Redundant unique indexes on core CMS tables are removed where a UX_* constraint covers the same logical columns, and remaining IX_* objects are non-unique lookup indexes.
PostgreSQL defaults remain unnamed.

description: Update CMS PostgreSQL seed and tenant-alter scripts to preserve fresh-install idempotency with quoted identifiers and the new constraint/index names.
acceptance-criteria: Seed scripts that insert default claims, authorization strategies, OpenIddict client data, and related configuration use quoted PascalCase dmscs table and column names.
TenantId add-column checks use information_schema or catalog predicates that match quoted PascalCase table and column names.
Tenant foreign keys use guarded FK_Table_Tenant constraints and the intended ON DELETE behavior.
Tenant lookup indexes are named IX_Table_TenantId and are non-unique unless they represent logical uniqueness.
The scripts can be run more than once against a fresh quoted install without duplicate-object failures.

description: Normalize CMS PostgreSQL OpenIddict install scripts and related storage tables to the same quoted identifier and constraint/index naming conventions.
acceptance-criteria: OpenIddictApplication, OpenIddictAuthorization, OpenIddictScope, OpenIddictApplicationScope, OpenIddictToken, OpenIddictKey, and OpenIddict role/claim tables use quoted PascalCase dmscs identifiers in install SQL.
OpenIddict primary keys and logical unique constraints are explicitly named with PK_* and UX_* constraints.
OpenIddict lookup indexes are non-unique IX_* indexes and no duplicate unique index remains where a UX_* constraint covers the same columns.
Foreign keys between OpenIddict tables and CMS-owned tables use guarded FK_* constraints with quoted references.
OpenIddict script comments reference quoted table and column names.

description: Update CMS PostgreSQL repository SQL to use quoted PascalCase dmscs identifiers and the new constraint names.
acceptance-criteria: All SQL in src/config/backend/EdFi.DmsConfigurationService.Backend.Postgresql/Repositories uses quoted PascalCase schema, table, column, alias-projected column, and ORDER BY identifiers.
Tenant filtering fragments and repository joins reference quoted PascalCase table and column names.
Ordering maps and paging SQL continue to produce valid PostgreSQL with quoted identifiers.
Duplicate-key handling checks for the new UX_* names and preserves existing API error behavior.
No repository SQL depends on existing lowercase dmscs table or column names.

description: Update CMS PostgreSQL OpenIddict repository SQL to use quoted PascalCase identifiers and the new UX_* duplicate-key names.
acceptance-criteria: OpenIddictDataRepository and OpenIddictTokenRepository SQL uses quoted PascalCase dmscs table and column names.
OpenIddict query filters, joins, ordering, inserts, updates, and deletes work against fresh quoted-schema installs.
OpenIddict duplicate-key and uniqueness handling references the explicit UX_* constraints introduced in the install scripts.
No OpenIddict repository SQL depends on lowercase dmscs objects.

description: Add focused CMS PostgreSQL database-shape tests for quoted PascalCase identifiers and DMS-style constraint/index names.
acceptance-criteria: Integration tests assert dmscs tables and columns exist with PascalCase names and do not exist only as folded lowercase names after a fresh install.
Integration tests assert PK_*, FK_*, UX_*, IX_*, and CK_* names for representative core, tenant-owned, and OpenIddict tables.
Integration tests assert logical uniqueness is represented by UX_* constraints and redundant unique IX_* indexes are absent.
Integration tests cover tenant-scoped uniqueness where repository behavior is tenant-scoped and global uniqueness where the domain is global.
Integration tests verify the install scripts can be run twice against the same fresh quoted database.

description: Change DMS tracked-change value-column derivation to remove only the Old/New prefix separator in PostgreSQL and SQL Server models.
acceptance-criteria: Tracked-change value columns are derived as OldX and NewX rather than Old_X and New_X.
Internal underscores in source column names are preserved, for example Old_Student_DocumentId becomes OldStudent_DocumentId.
Identifier shortening applies to the new Old/New-prefixed names without reintroducing the prefix separator.
Relational model manifests serialize the new tracked-change column names for both PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
No duplicate tracked-change value column names are introduced by the naming change.

description: Update DMS DDL emitters and trigger generation to use the new tracked-change column names in generated PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL.
acceptance-criteria: Tracked-change CREATE TABLE DDL emits OldX and NewX value columns in PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
Delete and key-change trigger bodies insert and select the new tracked-change column names.
Descriptor tracked-change tables and concrete resource tracked-change tables use the same naming rule.
Authorization support objects that join tracked_changes_* tables use the new OldX column names.
Expected DDL tests distinguish allowed explanatory comments from executable SQL that still uses Old_ or New_ prefixes.

description: Update tracked-change query planning, authorization SQL, and response mapping to consume the new OldX/NewX column names.
acceptance-criteria: TrackedChangeQueryPlanner SQL for deletes and keyChanges references OldX and NewX columns in PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
ReadChanges authorization SQL and static authorization-view definitions reference OldX tracked-change columns.
ChangeQueryResponseFieldMapper maps the new value-column names back to public oldKeyValues and newKeyValues fields without changing API response shapes.
RelationalChangeQueryRepository tests and seed SQL use the new column names.
Runtime behavior remains unchanged except for the database identifier names.

description: Quote the generated PostgreSQL GetMaxChangeVersion function name and add dialect-specific runtime calls for newest change version.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL generated DDL creates "dms"."GetMaxChangeVersion"() and no lowercase compatibility wrapper.
PostgreSQL generated DDL and tests call SELECT "dms"."GetMaxChangeVersion"().
SQL Server generated DDL and tests continue to use dms.GetMaxChangeVersion() or [dms].[GetMaxChangeVersion] as appropriate.
RelationalChangeQueryRepository no longer uses one shared SELECT dms.GetMaxChangeVersion() SQL string for both dialects.
A small dialect/provider abstraction selects the PostgreSQL quoted call and SQL Server unquoted/bracketed call without leaking PostgreSQL syntax into SQL Server paths.

description: Refresh affected generated DDL, manifest, and integration fixture artifacts for PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
acceptance-criteria: Authoritative ds-5.2, ds-5.2-tpdm, sample, focused, small, and integration fixture expected SQL files reflect quoted PostgreSQL GetMaxChangeVersion and OldX/NewX tracked-change columns.
Expected relational-model and ddl manifests reflect the new tracked-change value-column names and unchanged public response mappings.
Fixture updates are limited to generated identifier/name changes required by this story unless a test failure exposes a true dependent change.
PostgreSQL and SQL Server fixture outputs remain deterministic across repeated generation.

description: Update backend redesign reference docs affected by the DMS-1244 identifier convention changes.
acceptance-criteria: change-queries.md describes tracked-change value columns with OldX and NewX prefixes while preserving internal source-name underscores.
Tracked-change authorization, /deletes, and /keyChanges SQL examples in change-queries.md use OldX/NewX storage names or explicitly label any Old_/New_ text as historical explanation.
summary.md, ddl-generation.md, update-tracking.md, and change-queries.md reference the PostgreSQL function as "dms"."GetMaxChangeVersion"() where PostgreSQL identifier quoting matters.
Documentation updates are limited to DMS-1244 naming changes and do not rewrite unrelated backend redesign content.
steps-to-verify: Review remaining matches to confirm they are explanatory historical references rather than current generated identifier guidance.

description: Update and extend DMS integration tests for tracked-change behavior and PostgreSQL function-name quoting.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL generated-DDL smoke tests assert tracked-change rows use OldX/NewX columns and call "dms"."GetMaxChangeVersion"().
SQL Server generated-DDL smoke tests assert tracked-change rows use OldX/NewX columns while preserving SQL Server GetMaxChangeVersion calls.
RelationalChangeQueryRepository integration tests seed and query tracked-change tables with the new value-column names in both dialects.
Descriptor, delete, key-change, authorization, and identity-propagation tracked-change scenarios still pass in both dialects.
Tests cover at least one source column with internal underscores to verify only the prefix separator was removed.

description: Run final formatting, static guardrails, and focused DMS/CMS test coverage for DMS-1244.
acceptance-criteria: C# files changed for this story are formatted with csharpier.

Executable CMS PostgreSQL SQL no longer contains unquoted dmscs.Table references, lowercase folded table checks, lowercase pk_/fk_/uq_/idx_ constraint or index names, or duplicate unique-index patterns.
Executable DMS generated SQL, manifests, repository SQL, and SQL-asserting tests no longer use Old_ or New_ tracked-change prefixes except explanatory text that explicitly documents the rename.
PostgreSQL generated DDL no longer emits or calls "dms".GetMaxChangeVersion().
Backend redesign reference docs no longer present Old_/New_ tracked-change columns or unquoted PostgreSQL GetMaxChangeVersion calls as the current convention.
DMS Docker/env/docs/rest-client cache configuration no longer references CacheExpirationMinutes or CACHE_EXPIRATION_MINUTES and consistently exposes the seconds-based settings.
Focused DMS and CMS unit, integration, and E2E coverage relevant to identifier naming, tracked changes, and availableChangeVersions passes or any environmental skip/failure is documented.
